plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-552 RP18.P01.M02.S09-RP18.P01.M04.S06 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-552 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP18.P01 implementation slice: the M02 Type And Shape Definition tail (the twelve remaining rows), the full M03 Primary Implementation Slice cycle (twenty-two rows), and the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice head (the first six rows) as three descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H18), Claude review packet (C18), and CP00-553 handoff, without opening AI legal workflows, precedent, clause markup, or DD extraction runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary:
CP00-552 is a clean, descriptor-only AI Legal Workflows (RP18) p01 implementation-slice pack covering 40 units (RP18.P01.M02.S09-RP18.P01.M04.S06). Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is honest and supported: manifest and command-evidence both record production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, implementation_layer:descriptor, and a descriptor-verification production_ready_flag only — no false enterprise-trust or runtime claim.

Consistency verified end-to-end: deliverable distribution {implementation:23, ui:5, fixture:2, test:6, hermes_evidence:2, claude_review:2}=40 counted directly from included_units matches the manifest delivery_counts, contract cp552_requirements.deliverable_counts, and registry requirements; micro distribution (M02:12/M03:22/M04:6) matches. Contract current_pack, program.current_pack_id, p01_implementation_slice_descriptor projection, cp552_requirements, both no_write_attestation blocks, and historical_pack_bindings tail all pin CP00-552. README #cp00-552 anchor exists for the documentation_entry reference.

No-write/no-real-data boundary confirmed by source inspection: no writeFile/exec/spawn/fetch/child_process/network operations in the package or validator — only read-only readFile(Sync). Authority boundaries preserved everywhere (claude_is_final_approval:false; promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false; claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false). Hermes evidence packets are pure metadata (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false); Hermes is not embedded in product runtime.

Review hardening satisfied: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1434-byte success stdout — not zero-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped), tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob, forbidden_tools_present:false, no prior invalid review attempts, schema and prompt present and well-formed. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present, which is the correct pre-review state for this pending read-only run, not a defect. Recorded validation/command exit codes are all 0 and are corroborated by source/contract/plan/manifest consistency. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout, and the pack does not weaken any production_ready gate. Final closeout still requires the documented human approval step.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-552 ai legal workflows core p01 implementation slice descriptor pack
